fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arkansas SLP License Requirements — Fees, Board Contact & How to Apply (2026)
seo.description: Complete guide to Arkansas speech-language pathologist licensing. ABESPA application fees, Praxis requirements, and telepractice rules for 2026.
seo.keywords: Arkansas SLP license, speech-language pathologist Arkansas, Arkansas speech therapy license

board.name: Arkansas Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.mailing_address: 101 E. Capitol Ave., Suite 211, Little Rock, AR 72201
board.physical_address: 101 E. Capitol Ave., Suite 211, Little Rock, AR 72201

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Arkansas.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship under supervision.

steps.title: Earn a Master's Degree in SLP
steps.description: Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331, score 162).

steps.title: Complete Clinical Fellowship
steps.description: Complete 36 weeks of full-time supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

steps.title: Submit Application to the Board
steps.description: Submit application with $75 fee plus $25 license fee. Include transcripts, Praxis scores, and CF verification.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

telepractice_text: Arkansas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Arkansas issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Arkansas is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Arkansas through a compact privilege.

faqs.question: How much does an Arkansas SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost is approximately $100. Annual renewal is $75. Note that Arkansas requires annual rather than biennial renewal.

faqs.question: Is Arkansas part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arkansas has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

faqs.question: Does Arkansas allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arkansas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.
